chief_complaint: A 51-year-old male patient presented with a near-fatal diffuse alveolar haemorrhage and an acute myocardial infarction.
history: The patient had severe eosinophilic asthma, peripheral blood eosinophilia, and ear, nose, and throat involvement. He was treated with benralizumab (30 mg every 8 weeks) and oral corticosteroids. During tapering of corticosteroids, he developed diffuse alveolar haemorrhage as a manifestation of overt vasculitis.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Elevated troponin T levels, diffuse alveolar haemorrhage, and occlusion of the left anterior descending (LAD) artery.
correct_diagnosis: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis (EGPA) with acute myocardial infarction
differentials: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis (EGPA) with acute myocardial infarction
Acute myocardial infarction due to coronary artery disease
Diffuse alveolar haemorrhage due to vasculitis
correct_ranking: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis (EGPA) with acute myocardial infarction
Acute myocardial infarction due to coronary artery disease
Diffuse alveolar haemorrhage due to vasculitis
